folio.label: 50r
folio.l: The wyf cam lepyngö inward , with a renne

note.notetxt: 159 the ink on this folio is light brown, probably the same ink of the end of folio 49v .ODR

folio.l: She seide allas , ©oure hors goth to the fenne
With wylde Mares , as fast as he may go
Vnthank come on his hand , æ bond hi~ so
And he æ betir , shuld han knyt the reyne
Allas q¶ Ioún , Aleyn for cristes peyne
Lay doun thy swerd , and I wyl myn alswa
I is ful wygút , god wayt as is a raa
By goddes hert , he shal nat skape vs bathe
Why ne had thow put , the capul in the lathe
Il hayl , by god Aleyn , thow is a fonne
¢ Theise sely Clerkes , han ful fast I ronne
Toward the fen , bothe Aleyn & Ioún~
And wha~ne the Millere saw , æ they weren gon
He half a busshel , of here flour hath take
And bad his wyf , go knede it in a kake
He seide I trowe , the Clerkes ben a ferd
ñet can a Millere , make a Clerkes berd
For al his art , ©a lat hem gon here wey
Lo where they go , ©e late the children pley
They get hi~ nat , so ligútly , be my crounö
Theise sely Clerkes , rennen vp and dounö
With kepe kepe , stand stand , Iossa warderere
Ga , whistel thow , and I shal kepe hi~ here
But shortly , til it was verray nygút
They coude nat , thougú they dede al here mygút
Here capel cacche , he ran alwey so fast
Til in a diche , they cached hi~ at the last
Wery and wete , as beest is in the reyn
Cometh sely Ioún , & witú hi~ cometú Aleyn
¢ Allas q¶ Ioún , that day that I was born~
Now ar we dreuyn til hethyngö , and to skornö
Oure cornö is stole , men wyl vs fonnes calle
Bothe the wardeyn , and oure felawes alle
And namely the Millere , weylawey
Thus pleyneth Ioúnö , as he gotú by the wey
Toward the Mille , and bayard in his hond
The Millere sittyngö by the fyr , he fond
For it was nygút , and ferthere mygút æei nougút

note.notetxt: 197There is a small pricking in the right margin just above the one made to rule the frame. This pricking is present in all the following pages to f.58r. Perhaps it linked to the length of an exemplar or Dd as a copytext. On the other half of the quire from f. 62, there are two prickings: one is positioned as the one starting here, visible until f. 66; the other is just two lines underneath until f.67. NB: end of ML and begins WBP.ODR

folio.l: But for the loue of god , they hi~ bysougút
Of herberwe & of ease , as for here peny
¢ The Millere seide a geyn , if here be eny
Swicú as it is , ©et shuln ©e han ©oure part
Myn hous is streyt , but ©e han lerned art
ñe conne by argumentez , maken a place
